schema_description: Structured field schema for extracting key estate planning details from a prospect's existing trust documents. Designed so an attorney can quickly understand the prospect's current plan before a meeting.

sections.name: Trust Overview
sections.description: High-level identification of the trust instrument, its type, and governing details.

fields.name: Grantor
fields.description: The grantor(s) / settlor(s) / trustor(s) who created the trust.

fields.name: Trust Type
fields.description: Whether the trust is revocable or irrevocable.

fields.name: Date Signed
fields.description: The date(s) the trust documents were signed or last restated/amended.

fields.name: Governing Law
fields.description: The state or jurisdiction whose law governs the trust.

sections.name: Beneficiaries and Distributions
sections.description: Who benefits from the trust, how assets are distributed, and any GST provisions.

fields.name: Current Beneficiaries
fields.description: The current beneficiaries of the trust (e.g. Grantor, Grantor and Spouse, children, etc.).

fields.name: Formula for Funding Upon First Death
fields.description: How trust assets are allocated or divided upon the first spouse's death (e.g. credit shelter, marital deduction, disclaimer, fractional, pecuniary).

fields.name: Specific Distributions
fields.description: Any specific gifts, bequests, or tangible personal property distributions specified in the documents.

fields.name: GST Status
fields.description: Whether the trust is GST-exempt, non-exempt, or partially exempt based on the allocation of GST exemption described in the documents.

fields.name: GST Provisions
fields.description: Generation-skipping transfer tax provisions, allocations, or exemption planning described in the documents.

sections.name: Trust Structure
sections.description: How separate trusts are structured after the triggering event and what powers beneficiaries hold.

fields.name: Structure of Separate Trusts
fields.description: How separate/successor trusts are structured: whether distributions are outright or held in further trust, withdrawal powers (e.g. 5-and-5), powers of appointment (limited or general), and trust termination provisions.

sections.name: Fiduciaries
sections.description: Trustees, agents, and protectors named in the documents.

fields.name: Trustees / Agents
fields.description: The trustees (initial, successor, disability) and any agents (POA, healthcare surrogate, personal representative) named in the documents.

fields.name: Trust Protector
fields.description: The trust protector, if any, and a brief description of their powers if stated.

sections.name: Other Notes
sections.description: Any other notable provisions or details an attorney should be aware of.

fields.name: Other Notes
fields.description: Any other key details, notable provisions, unusual clauses, or important context not captured in the fields above that an attorney should know before a meeting.
